exampleSentencesTranslated: I want to travel to America next summer.
My older sister has been studying at a university in America since last year.
His parents lived in America for more than ten years before returning to Vietnam.
notes_md:
**Mỹ** = America, usually meaning the United States. It’s a proper noun, so capitalize it: **Mỹ**.

Often used after prepositions like **ở, sang, từ** when talking about location or movement.  

It also works like an adjective before a noun: **người Mỹ, văn hóa Mỹ, phim Mỹ**.  

Be careful: **mỹ** (lowercase) inside words like **mỹ thuật, thẩm mỹ** means “beautiful/aesthetic” — different meaning, same sound.

content.sentence: Anh muốn đi du lịch ở ___.

translation.en: I want to travel to America.

exampleSentences: Anh muốn đi du lịch ở Mỹ vào mùa hè năm sau.
Chị gái em đang học đại học tại Mỹ từ năm ngoái.
Bố mẹ anh từng sống ở Mỹ hơn mười năm trước khi về Việt Nam.

translation.en: I want to travel to America.

content.sentence: Anh muốn đi du lịch ở ___.

example.en: I want to go to America.

notes:
Mỹ = America, usually meaning the United States. It’s a proper noun, so capitalize it: Mỹ.

Often used after prepositions like ở, sang, từ when talking about location or movement.  

It also works like an adjective before a noun: người Mỹ, văn hóa Mỹ, phim Mỹ.  

Be careful: mỹ (lowercase) inside words like mỹ thuật, thẩm mỹ means “beautiful/aesthetic” — different meaning, same sound.
